scenarios.label: AKL — Blade/Remote Head (OBD)
scenarios.tips: AKL requires PIN extraction — not all vehicles support OBD PIN read. Check IM608 coverage before committing to job. On Toyota/Nissan: confirm NATS/smart key generation. If PIN read fails, escalate to EEPROM. PDF use-case mapping: all-keys-lost should prefer OEM security-access paths wherever available; aftermarket AKL workflows carry higher legal and bricking risk. Profile / reader support: Keyline Camillo Bianchi Reader Credential prerequisites often apply: NASTF VSP (US) and/or SERMI (EU) are listed in the PDF as security-access frameworks, not programming tools.

scenarios.label: AKL — EEPROM / Bench Method
scenarios.tips: EEPROM work on mobile van should be limited to accessible modules (BCM, instrument cluster, ECU). Do not attempt on encapsulated/potted modules on-site — tow to workshop. Always photograph module location and connector orientation before removal. Backup EEPROM image before writing. PDF use-case mapping: bench/EEPROM path is for authorised repair/data migration when OBD/OEM routines are unavailable or failed; highest bricking risk in this workbook. Profile / reader support: Keyline Camillo Bianchi Reader Credential prerequisites often apply: NASTF VSP (US) and/or SERMI (EU) are listed in the PDF as security-access frameworks, not programming tools. ECU/TCU escalation tools from the PDF: Alientech KESS3 | Magicmotorsport Flex | Dimsport New Genius | AutoTuner Tool | CMDFlash (Flashtec).

scenarios.label: AKL — Smart/Proximity (OBD)
scenarios.tips: High-risk job. Confirm vehicle is not in anti-theft lockout before starting. On Hyundai/Kia NX4/NQ5 era: confirm Immo V compatibility with IM608 before quoting. On Toyota DST AES (LC300, Prado 250): may require Lonsdor K518 backup or dealer escalation. PDF use-case mapping: all-keys-lost smart-key work should prefer OEM security access; remote prep and secure pairing remain core supporting layers. Profile / reader support: Keyline Camillo Bianchi Reader Credential prerequisites often apply: NASTF VSP (US) and/or SERMI (EU) are listed in the PDF as security-access frameworks, not programming tools.

scenarios.label: Add Key — Blade/Remote Head
scenarios.tips: Always confirm key count and anti-theft status before cutting. On Ford PATS: check for max key limit (8 keys). On Toyota: confirm G-chip vs 4D before cutting — wrong chip = wasted blank. PDF use-case mapping: spare key addition is best served by OBD key programming tablets or OEM scan toolchains; transponder/remote prep and cutting tools are supporting layers. Profile / reader support: Keyline Camillo Bianchi Reader

scenarios.label: Add Key — Smart / Proximity
scenarios.tips: Smart keys cannot be cloned — OBD programming only. Confirm immobiliser family before ordering blank — wrong shell will not program. Universal smart keys (VVDI XS series) are cost-effective but confirm fitment first. PDF use-case mapping: smart/proximity add-key jobs use OBD/OEM programming plus remote preparation and optional emergency-blade cutting. Profile / reader support: Keyline Camillo Bianchi Reader

scenarios.label: BCM / ECU Reset + Key Relearn
scenarios.tips: Confirm new BCM/ECU is programmed to vehicle VIN before relearn — aftermarket modules often need VIN flash first. On GM/Holden VE-VF: BCM replacement is common — IM608 handles well but VIN must match. On Ford: PCM/BCM must be pre-configured to vehicle. On BMW: CAS replacement requires EEPROM transfer or dealer-level tool. PDF use-case mapping: module replacement/relearn work should prefer OEM toolchains first; bench/ECU tools are escalation paths for authorised repair contexts. Credential prerequisites often apply: NASTF VSP (US) and/or SERMI (EU) are listed in the PDF as security-access frameworks, not programming tools. Advanced validation tools from the PDF: Vector CANalyzer | Vector CANoe | Kvaser Leaf Light HS v2 | PEAK PCAN-USB Pro FD | PEAK PCAN-USB FD.

scenarios.label: Flip Key / Switchblade Programming
scenarios.tips: Assembly order: chip first, then blade, then remote component, then close shell and test. Confirm spring tension before handing to customer — loose blade is a common comeback. Source shells from LSC or Xhorse VVDI shell range. PDF use-case mapping: flip-key work combines cutting, remote generation, transponder preparation, and vehicle-side pairing. Profile / reader support: Keyline Camillo Bianchi Reader

scenarios.label: High-Security Laser / Track Key
scenarios.tips: Futura Pro is the best tool for laser/track accuracy — always preferred over Dolphin for these profiles. On BMW: confirm CAS generation (EWS3 vs CAS2 vs CAS3+ vs FEM/BDC) before committing — different blank AND different programming workflow. HU66 cuts for VAG are very common — keep HU66 blank stock high. PDF use-case mapping: high-security blade work depends first on cutter/clamp/database capability, then on IMMO/remote programming if electronics are involved. Profile / reader support: Keyline Camillo Bianchi Reader

scenarios.label: IMMO Fault Diagnosis (no-start)
scenarios.tips: Common fault tree: (1) Check chip type matches vehicle — wrong chip looks identical. (2) Test antenna ring continuity — breaks are common on steering column wear. (3) Confirm ECU/BCM is not in anti-theft lockout. (4) Check for water damage at BCM connector. Do not replace parts until fault is confirmed — antenna rings are cheap, ECUs are not. PDF use-case mapping: no-start diagnosis spans diagnostics, OEM/J2534 programming, network analysis, RF troubleshooting, and sometimes authorised bench verification. Credential prerequisites often apply: NASTF VSP (US) and/or SERMI (EU) are listed in the PDF as security-access frameworks, not programming tools. Advanced diagnostic tools from the PDF: Vector CANalyzer | Vector CANoe | Kvaser Leaf Light HS v2 | PEAK PCAN-USB Pro FD | PEAK PCAN-USB FD | RF Explorer family | tinySA / tinySA Ultra | Rigol DSA800 series | Keysight FieldFox family.

scenarios.label: Ignition Lock / Barrel Replacement
scenarios.tips: Always read/confirm transponder chip type BEFORE removing barrel — easier to verify with chip in place. Photograph barrel connector orientation before disconnection. On older vehicles (pre-2000): barrel replacement may solve immobiliser issues if chip embedded in barrel rather than key. PDF use-case mapping: barrel replacement is primarily a cutting/profile workflow, with IMMO relearn tools needed when an electronic key must be reintroduced. Profile / reader support: Keyline Camillo Bianchi Reader

scenarios.label: Motorcycle / Powersports Key
scenarios.tips: Motorcycle IMMO coverage is significantly thinner than automotive. Honda CBR/PCX/Africa Twin (smart key Honda) requires specialist approach. Always confirm IM608 MC coverage for specific model before quoting AKL on motorcycle. PDF is car-focused, so mapped tools here are generic IMMO/cutting candidates only; verify exact powersports support by make/model before committing. Profile / reader support: Keyline Camillo Bianchi Reader

scenarios.label: Remote / FOB Programming Only
scenarios.tips: Confirm remote frequency before generating/ordering — AU-spec vs import vehicles differ (315MHz Japan imports vs 433MHz AU-delivered). Always test with Key Tool Max Pro first. VVDI universal remotes save on blank stock but confirm chip compatibility. PDF use-case mapping: remote-only jobs use remote pre-coding/generation tools plus vehicle-side pairing; RF analysers help diagnose interference/range problems. RF troubleshooting tools from the PDF: RF Explorer family | tinySA / tinySA Ultra | Rigol DSA800 series | Keysight FieldFox family.

scenarios.label: Transponder Clone / Chip Copy
scenarios.tips: ID46 (PCF7936) can be cloned in most cases. ID48 (PCF7941) requires crypto key — Zed-Full handles most. PCF7953/4A/8A (modern smart systems) CANNOT be cloned — must do OBD programming. Always verify chip family before quoting clone job. PDF use-case mapping: cloning is chip-family dependent; many tools will explicitly report when a chip cannot be copied. Profile / reader support: Keyline Camillo Bianchi Reader
